# Architecture

## Overview

toktra now targets a five-layer realtime translation architecture. The product keeps the original bilingual rendering model, but turns page translation into an explicit pipeline: collect segments, schedule work, translate through a provider, render safely, then improve coverage with rules and AI structure strategies.

## Module Boundaries

| Module | Responsibility | Depends on | Public contract |
|---|---|---|---|
| Segmenter | Convert visible DOM modules into stable segment objects with metadata. | DOM, `core.js` text filters | `collectTargets`, `createSegment`, `segmentId`, `kind`, `priority` |
| Scheduler | Decide what to render from cache and what to send to translation, ordered for reading experience. | Segmenter output, memory cache | `planTranslationWork(targets, options)` |
| Provider | Translate either text arrays or segment requests and namespace cache by provider/model/prompt/language. | background service worker, storage, fetch | `translateTexts`, `translateSegments`, provider registry |
| Render | Insert bilingual translations, translate placeholders safely, expose progress state, cleanly remove UI. | DOM, segment metadata | `renderTranslation`, `renderProgress`, `removeTranslations` |
| Rule/AI Strategy | Merge user rules and AI domain strategy as supplemental selectors, without replacing default walker. | settings, cached strategies, core parser | `parseSiteRules`, `mergeStructureStrategies` |

## Data Model

```text
SegmentTarget {
  id: string
  element: Element
  text: string
  hash: string
  kind: heading | paragraph | list | link | button | placeholder | table | block | inline
  priority: number
  position: { index, top, left, hasLayout }
}

TranslationRequest {
  id: string
  text: string
  hash: string
  kind: string
  context?: string
}

TranslationResult {
  id: string
  text: string
  cacheHit: boolean
}
```

## API / Interface Contracts

- Content script may send `TOKTRA_TRANSLATE` with legacy `texts` or new `segments`.
- Background returns legacy `translations` plus optional `results`.
- Cache keys include provider namespace, source/target language, model, prompt version, and normalized text hash.
- Rules use an uBlock-inspired subset: `domain##selector` excludes; `domain#+#selector` includes.

## Error Handling

- Missing API key stays a setup error before network calls.
- Bad provider responses preserve existing error codes.
- Rule parse ignores malformed or unsafe selectors.
- Progress UI reports failures without blocking removal or rescans.

## Security and Privacy

- No API key is written to sync storage.
- Input fields are not read or modified except static placeholders.
- The default provider remains OpenAI-compatible; future local providers can be added behind the same interface.

## Deployment Shape

Chrome MV3 extension loaded from `extension/`. Unit tests use Node's built-in test runner and `linkedom`; extension static checks use `scripts/check-extension.js`.

## Architecture Decisions

- Keep bilingual insertion instead of in-place replacement to avoid breaking app state and rich HTML alignment.
- Use rules and AI strategy as additive guidance, not a replacement for the fast default walker.
- Keep the Provider layer small and data-oriented so Chrome built-in Translator API or local engines can be added later.
